column1: # ZAP alert filter — accepted risks and confirmed false positives.
# Format (tab-separated): <alert_id>
# Actions: IGNORE (suppress entirely), WARN (log but don't fail), FAIL (fail workflow)
# This file is used by the scheduled ZAP baseline scan (.github/workflows/zap.yml).
# Assumes PR #402 has been merged (CORP, COOP, X-Powered-By, img-src, form-action fixed).
# Remaining suppressed findings are either structural Next.js constraints, reverse-proxy
# responsibilities, or confirmed false positives — each documented below.
# CSP: script-src unsafe-inline / style-src unsafe-inline / connect-src https:
# unsafe-inline is a Next.js App Router requirement (inline hydration scripts and Tailwind styles).
# connect-src https: is intentionally broad — LLM base URLs are user-configured at runtime.
# Both are documented accepted risks; removing them would require significant rework.
# Proxy Disclosure (TRACE / OPTIONS methods leaking nginx presence)
# This is a Synology reverse-proxy config issue, not an application fix.
# Mitigation: disable TRACE in nginx upstream config.
# HSTS (Strict-Transport-Security) not set
# Intentionally excluded from the app — HSTS set in-app would permanently break
# HTTP-only LAN deployments. Must be configured in the reverse proxy (nginx) only.
# See next.config.ts comment for full rationale.
# Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy (COEP) header missing
# require-corp would break blob: TTS audio (URL.createObjectURL) and WebRTC.
# COOP and CORP are set (PR #402); COEP is the only remaining 90004 finding.
# Cookie Slack Detector — false positive
# The thinkarr_session cookie correctly has no effect on /_next/static/* and other
# public routes. The middleware intentionally bypasses auth for these paths.
# Big Redirect Detected — false positive
# Next.js redirects / → /chat with a full HTML body; this is framework behaviour,
# not an information leak. The body contains no sensitive data.
# Informational — no action required
# These alerts are expected for any modern Next.js application.
